comment.body:
"Downside pivot is at 6827.60
and from there the profile is very supportive, especially into PM.
6817.47 is a reversion back up towards 6840/6850 and pin there, most likely.
Market guards downside quite well, bcs if this line would be broken by constant put skew bid, while spot is rangebounding at the downside target of 6817.47, 6800/6790 area is also supportive and downside vol of vol is again heavily sold. Tho not as heavy as upside vol of vol…? Alma's genius

comment.body:
US was made by europeans, and the majority of its greatest scientists were from Europe.
the thing is that in Europe the corruption works in a way that doesn't really support innovation. They don't care. They buy things from US and CN and RUS, and they are fine. 
And the scientists, artists everybody is forced to leave.
you can't get money for your projects, you literally need to look for mentors, investors to do that. That was one of the main reasons I jumped into making money by myself.

and i'm talking on any level. 
so, it is applied not only for the no-name, new and young scientists, but for the professional, succesfull ones too.
they don't support shit here.
only lmbtq and politics. nothing else.

not even artists.
you have to work 7/24 to be able to write a book and distribute it on your own spend,
if you are a musician, you can't get support, only if you are the cousin of the PM.
(my brother for example. he is a great composer, wrote three wonderful album. still could not record it. he does not accept money from me, and he does not earn enough to do it... he is trying for 7 years already!! sent a lot of request to the state, no response)
medicals, directors, actors, none...

comment.body: To answer your question in the other thread, Alma: in short, my situation is math academia. What you wrote was pretty true, about ego, curiosity, humility/arrogance and academicians etc.. (It's not unusual to encounter arrogant colleagues or those who fight over authorship/citations etc..) It was a good reminder that motivates me; to be honest, your work ethic/philosophy is on par with that of some of the best mentors/colleagues I have seen.

comment.body: @Alma I have a clarification question from weekly post . You mentioned that —> Net vanna is also positive. And this is very telling! Because this along with the others tells me, that market expects rather ‘volatility scalpings’ on the put side into FOMC, rather than real left-tail moves. Dealer positive Vanna coming from short puts below & long calls above ( risk reversal ) structure - are you stating any down drift moves will quickly get monetized by market participants thus not real left tail moves . Secondly you also mentioned - Vanna switches to negative above - which I’m assuming will dealers short calls or market part pants buying upside calls expecting realized vol - which would be spot up vol up scenario. Could you clarify -5% vol increase - is it VIX up 5% ?
